questions.question: In a right triangle, the side opposite a 35° angle is 7 cm and the hypotenuse is 12 cm. Which trigonometric ratio would you use to verify this relationship?
questions.explanation: The sine ratio compares the opposite side to the hypotenuse. Since we know the opposite side and hypotenuse, sin(θ) = opposite/hypotenuse applies here.

questions.question: A ladder leans against a wall at a 60° angle from the ground. If the ladder is 10 feet long, how high up the wall does it reach? Round to one decimal place.
questions.explanation: The height is the side opposite the 60° angle. Use sin(60°) = height/10, so height = 10 × sin(60°) = 10 × (√3/2) ≈ 8.7 feet.

questions.question: On the unit circle, what is the x-coordinate of the point at 90°?
questions.explanation: At 90°, the point is directly above the origin on the unit circle. The x-coordinate corresponds to cos(90°) = 0.

questions.question: In a right triangle, tan(θ) = 5/12. What is the value of tan(90° − θ)?
questions.explanation: By the cofunction identity, tan(90° − θ) = cot(θ) = 1/tan(θ) = 1/(5/12) = 12/5.

questions.question: A surveyor stands 50 meters from the base of a building and measures an angle of elevation of 40° to the top. What trigonometric ratio relates the height of the building to the known distance?
questions.explanation: The angle of elevation creates a right triangle where the adjacent side is 50 m. Tangent relates opposite (height) to adjacent: tan(40°) = height/50.

questions.question: On the unit circle, at what angle θ (between 0° and 360°) does sin(θ) = cos(θ)?
questions.explanation: sin(θ) = cos(θ) when tan(θ) = 1. This occurs at θ = 45° (first quadrant) and θ = 225° (third quadrant).
